vacancySearchResults.jobTitle: Positive Behaviour Implementation Coaches (Psychologists, Social Workers, Teachers, Occupational Therapists, Speech Pathologists) - Register of Interest
vacancySearchResults.section: Student Wellbeing and Inclusion Programs and Services, Client Services
vacancySearchResults.contactPerson: Jessica Nowak, Director Client Services on 0499 790 424 or jessica.nowak@education.nt.gov.au
vacancySearchResults.primaryObjective:
This  Register of Interest is for multiple Positive Behaviour Implementation Coach job opportunities available throughout the Northern Territory. Applications may be assessed while the Register remains open or immediately on its closure in July.

PBI Coaches build the capacity of educators to support the wellbeing and safety of students with diverse needs and maximise their educational outcomes. Positive Behaviour Implementation Coaches implement whole of schools approaches and use knowledge of wellbeing, safety and behaviour approaches to provide advice, coaching, mentoring and professional development to school leaders, teachers and support staff. 

Successful applicants must hold a tertiary qualification in education, social work, psychology, speech pathology or occupational therapy, and full registration or ability to register or obtain membership with, Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Authority, Australian Association of Social Work (AASW), Speech Pathology Australia, Northern Territory Teacher Registration Board, depending on relevant profession. Copies of your qualifications and proof of professional membership status must be provided for your application to be considered. 

Selection will be made at the P2 or P3 level depending on the experience/qualifications of successful applicants. Applicants may request to be based in various locations throughout the NT.
vacancySearchResults.specialInstructions:
Aboriginal applicants will be granted priority consideration for this vacancy.

Applicants must provide copies of their tertiary qualification in education, social work, psychology, speech pathology or occupational therapy, and full registration or ability to register or obtain membership with, Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Authority (AHPRA), Australian Association of Social Work (AASW), Speech Pathology Australia (SPA), Northern Territory Teacher Registration Board (TRB), depending on relevant profession. 

Work VISA documentation must be provided if you are not an Australian citizen.

vacancySearchResults.jobTitle: Inclusion Advisor Psychologist - Register of Interest
vacancySearchResults.section: Student Wellbeing and Inclusion Programs and Services - Client Services
vacancySearchResults.contactPerson: Susi Meyers, Assistant Director Client Services on 0499 873 225 or susanne.meyers@education.nt.gov.au
vacancySearchResults.primaryObjective:
This Register of Interest is for multiple Inclusion Advisor Psychologist job opportunities available throughout the Northern Territory. Applications may be assessed while the Register remains open or immediately on its closure in July.

Psychologists work within a Multi-tiered System of Support framework to build capacity of educators to support students to maximise their educational outcomes. School psychologists perform psychological assessments with students for various reasons using psychometric testing, observation and interviews with parents and teachers. Consultation, comprehensive assessment, treatment planning and short term therapeutic interventions are provided to students with learning, behaviour and personal/social competence concerns that may be having a direct impact on educational outcomes.

Successful applicants must hold a degree in psychology and registration as a psychologist with the Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency. Copies of your qualifications and proof of professional membership status must be provided for your application to be considered. 

Selection will be made at the P1, P2 or P3 level depending on available positions and the experience/qualifications of successful applicants. Applicants may request to be based in various locations throughout the NT.
vacancySearchResults.specialInstructions:
Aboriginal applicants will be granted priority consideration for this vacancy.

Special Instructions: Aboriginal applicants will be granted priority consideration for this vacancy. Applicants must provide copies of their degree in psychology and proof of registration as psychologist with the Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Authority (AHPRA) to be considered. Work VISA documentation must be provided if you are not an Australian citizen.

Further Information: https://boundlesspossible.nt.gov.au/about/the-territory
